---
title: "Assignment 4 Solution Key: Checking Model Assumptions"
author: "STAT-101"
date: "2026-02-10"
format: html
---

# Assignment 4: Checking Model Assumptions

## Setup

```{r setup}
#| echo: true
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, car, performance)
```

## Problem 1: Normality Check

```{r normality-check}
#| echo: true
set.seed(42)
sample_data <- rnorm(100, mean = 50, sd = 10)

# QQ-plot
qqnorm(sample_data)
qqline(sample_data, col = "red")

# Shapiro-Wilk test
shapiro.test(sample_data)
```

## Problem 2: Variance Homogeneity

```{r levene-test}
#| echo: true
groups <- rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 30)
values <- c(rnorm(30, 50, 10), rnorm(30, 52, 12), rnorm(30, 48, 11))
df <- data.frame(group = groups, value = values)

# Levene's test
car::leveneTest(value ~ group, data = df)
```

## Problem 3: Plot-only chunk

```{r diagnostic-plots}
#| echo: true
model <- lm(value ~ group, data = df)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(model)
```

## Problem 4: Skipped chunk (not evaluated)

```{r optional-advanced, eval=FALSE}
#| eval: false
# This chunk is for advanced students only
# Should be SKIPPED during validation
performance::check_model(model)
```
